Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — thanks again for taking the time yesterday. As discussed, ACME is preparing to onboard its first private deployment and we want to align on a recommended connectivity pattern that balances latency, operational ownership, and auditability.

I attached a short comparison (redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf) but high level the three paths we typically see are: 
- VPC peering (cross-account peering) — low-latency, simple routing but requires both sides to accept peering and route coordination.
- AWS PrivateLink (interface endpoints) — strong isolation and per-service endpoints, easier cross-account access controls, but intro...

2) What does an \"escrow\" or escrow-like approach look like for metadata and audit logs (legal wants an auditable copy retained in EU and a separate encrypted mirror in APAC)?

Elena — thanks again for the productive call earlier. I wanted to summarize what we captured and confirm our immediate next steps so we can get your security team the evidence they need quickly.

Key items from call (high level):
- PII scope: customer names, email, financial account identifiers, transaction IDs, and derived metadata (risk scores).

- Retention: default retention for transaction records is 7 years for compliance; some analytics indices can be shorter.

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Wu <sam.wu@nebula.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery: 30-minute product briefing — pick 2–3 slots

Hi Sam —

Thanks again for the time today. Great to hear how Nebula is thinking about inference cost and multi-region SLAs. Per our recap, next best step is a 30-minute product briefing focused on: demo of inference runtime, high-level cost levers (batching/caching/quantization), and the security checklist your team asked about.

Would you and your stakeholders be able to pick one of the following 30‑minute windows? If neither works, suggest two alternatives and we’ll iterate.

From: Sophie Lane <sophie.lane@brightlayer.ai>
To: chloe.martin@redwood.ai
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: AWS marketplace metering questions for BrightLayer

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the demo last week — our engineering team is excited about Redwood's batching and entitlement hooks. We're starting to scope the AWS Marketplace integration and have a couple of concrete questions before we formalize the mutual action plan: 

1) Entitlement identifier: can Redwood accept a customer-provided entitlement_id (UUID) from AWS fulfillment webhook and map it directly to our tenant id, or do we need to mint a Redwood-side entitlement as well?

2) Usage dimensions: we have two consumption metrics (API calls and compute-seconds). Does the Redwood metering adapter support multi-dimension usage reporting to AWS, or should we consolidate to a single normalized unit for Marketplace u...
